defs: A suffix indicating that the substance, in the name of which it appears, is a ketone; as, acetone.
A termination indicating that the hydrocarbon to the name of which it is affixed belongs to the fourth series of hydrocarbons, or the third series of unsaturated hydrocarbons; as, nonone.

records.ety: [OE. one, on, an, AS. ān; akin to D. een, OS. ēn, OFries. ēn, ān, G. ein, Dan. een, Sw. en, Icel. einn, Goth. ains, W. un, Ir. & Gael. aon, L. unus, earlier oinos, oenos, Gr. o'i`nh the ace on dice; cf. Skr. ēka. The same word as the indefinite article a, an. √ 299. Cf. 2d A, 1st An, Alone, Anon, Any, None, Nonce, Only, Onion, Unit.]

defs: Being a single unit, or entire being or thing, and no more; not multifold; single; individual.
quotes: The dream of Pharaoh is one.
O that we now had here But one ten thousand of those men in England.

defs: Denoting a person or thing conceived or spoken of indefinitely; a certain. “I am the sister of one Claudio” [Shak.], that is, of a certain man named Claudio.
Pointing out a contrast, or denoting a particular thing or person different from some other specified; -- used as a correlative adjective, with or without the.
quotes: From the one side of heaven unto the other.

defs: Closely bound together; undivided; united; constituting a whole.
quotes: The church is therefore one, though the members may be many.

defs: Single in kind; the same; a common.
quotes: One plague was on you all, and on your lords.
Men may counsel a woman to be one.
One day when Phoebe fair, With all her band, was following the chase.
Well, I will marry one day.
notes: ☞ One is often used in forming compound words, the meaning of which is obvious; as, one-armed, one-celled, one-eyed, one-handed, one-hearted, one-horned, one-idead, one-leaved, one-masted, one-ribbed, one-story, one-syllable, one-stringed, one-winged, etc.

defs: A single unit; as, one is the base of all numbers.
A symbol representing a unit, as 1, or i.

quotes: He will hate the one, and love the other.
That we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.

defs: Any person, indefinitely; a person or body; as, what one would have well done, one should do one's self.
quotes: It was well worth one's while.
Against this sort of condemnation one must steel one's self as one best can.
When any one heareth the word.
She knew every one who was any one in the land of Bohemia.
The Peloponnesians and the Athenians fought against one another.
notes: One is often used with some, any, no, each, every, such, a, many a, another, the other, etc. It is sometimes joined with another, to denote a reciprocal relation. [1913 Webster] When any one heareth the word. Matt. xiii. 19. [1913 Webster] She knew every one who was any one in the land of Bohemia. Compton Reade. [1913 Webster] The Peloponnesians and the Athenians fought against one another. Jowett (Thucyd. ). [1913 Webster] The gentry received one another. Thackeray. [1913 Webster]

defs: To cause to become one; to gather into a single whole; to unite; to assimilate.
quotes: The rich folk that embraced and oned all their heart to treasure of the world.
